body.title: Add palace node staleness detection to day-close-audit
body.result: palace-decay-detection SQL audit check registered as forever check; surfaces palace nodes not updated in 30+ days as LOW severity finding at day-close
body.description:
Palace nodes without recent verification are potential false confidence sources. A rule node claiming 'Postgres only' that was last verified 6 months ago may no longer reflect current practice. Add a day-close-audit step (Step 18.8) that surfaces nodes where updated_at > 30 days for spot-check. Distinct from capability_map.md drift check (step 18.6) — this applies to all palace branches, not just one doc.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md (new step 18.8), wild_igor/igor/tools/palace_decay.py (new)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database (Postgres query against memory_palace/adc.palace)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: detect nodes by age, emit findings report for Akien review. Out of scope: automated palace updates — Akien approves all changes.
**Test plan:** Insert a test palace node with updated_at > 30 days; run step; verify it appears in findings. Verify nodes < 30 days are suppressed.
